data_source_summary: PM-KISAN Central Notification (December 1, 2018); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26 (national-state coordination); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; pmkisan.gov.in portal; agriportal.cg.nic.in

Q2.v: Agriculture Department, Government of Chhattisgarh (state-level implementation: farmer database maintenance, payment coordination, grievance resolution); Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Government of India (central scheme owner)
Q2.src: PM-KISAN Official Notification; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26

Q3.v: PM-KISAN Central Notification dated December 1, 2018; PM-KISAN Operational Guidelines (MoAFW); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25
Q3.src: programs.md — ANNOUNCEMENT, SURVEY, CAG, BUDGET entries

Q4.r: PM-KISAN is a Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) scheme that provides ₹6,000/year income support to 'all landholding farmer families.' The primary resource underpinning the scheme is private agricultural land held by individual farmer households. The scheme delivers cash directly to private bank accounts with no commons resource as the operational basis. There is no community-governed, publicly notified, or collectively stewarded resource involved. T1.1 = C (private land/privately owned property) → AUTOMATIC TEST 1 FAIL.
Q4.src: PM-KISAN Official Notification December 1, 2018 (eligibility clause: 'all landholding farmer families'); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q5.v: The primary resource is private agricultural land held by 32.4 lakh individual farming families across all 28 districts of Chhattisgarh. The scheme provides ₹6,000/year (3 instalments of ₹2,000 each) directly to private farmer bank accounts via DBT. There is no commons resource — no common land, water body, watershed, community institution, or knowledge commons — that serves as the operational basis of the scheme. All benefits flow exclusively to private landholding farmer households. Scheme is operational in all 28 districts of Chhattisgarh.
Q5.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; pmkisan.gov.in

Q6.r: There is no commons resource in this scheme, hence no commons boundary to demarcate. The private agricultural landholdings of 32.4 lakh farmers are the resource base; individual land records (Khasra/Khatauni) exist for private holdings but these are not a commons boundary. No GP resolution, watershed map, survey number, or community boundary document demarcating a commons exists for this scheme.
Q6.src: PM-KISAN Official Notification; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26

Q7.r: PM-KISAN targets private landholding farmers — not a community exercising collective access to a shared resource. There is no community access or practice right to a commons because no commons exists in this scheme. Score = 0: purely administrative/policy entitlement to private individuals based on land ownership records. No legal, statutory, or customary basis for community access to a shared resource exists.
Q7.src: PM-KISAN Official Notification (eligibility: 'all landholding farmer families'); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q8.r: No active diversion order, mining lease, change-of-land-use notification, SEZ notification, or compulsory acquisition proceeding affecting a commons has been identified in relation to this scheme. However, this question is effectively moot as the scheme operates on private agricultural land, not a commons. No active legal encumbrance overriding community access to any commons resource has been found.
Q8.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26; pmkisan.gov.in

Q9.breakdown: Entire expenditure = Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) cash payments to 32.4 lakh farmer bank accounts at ₹6,000/farmer/year. No administrative or infrastructure spend component separately identified in state-level data. Central allocation FY 2025-26 estimate: ₹1,280 Cr.
Q9.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (₹1,220 Cr direct benefit transfer); Outcome Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26

Q10.r: The entire scheme expenditure of ₹1,220 Cr is disbursed as Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) cash payments to private individual farmer bank accounts. Zero expenditure flows to any common/public/community resource — no common land improvement, no water body, no watershed, no community institution, no knowledge commons, no community infrastructure. All outputs flow exclusively to private individuals.
Q10.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; PM-KISAN Official Notification

Q11.r: ₹1,220 Cr = total DBT to 32.4 lakh private farmer households. The entire scheme expenditure benefits only private individuals (registered landholding farmers). Q10 + Q11 = 0 + 1220 = 1220 = Q9. Verified consistent.
Q11.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26

Q12.interp: FAIL — θ₁ = 0.00, far below the pass threshold of ≥ 0.50. The entire scheme expenditure is private DBT with zero allocation to commons.
Q12.src: Derived from CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 and PM-KISAN scheme structure

CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — PM-KISAN fund flow and DBT audit
Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 (PM-KISAN fund flow coordination)
pmkisan.gov.in — portal beneficiary statistics (32.4 lakh, ₹1,220 Cr)

Q14.r: Benefits are fully excludable. Only registered landholding farmers who meet PM-KISAN eligibility criteria (land ownership, income threshold compliance, Aadhaar/bank linkage) receive payments. Non-landholders, landless agricultural labourers, ineligible income-category households, and all community members not in the beneficiary database are fully excluded. The scheme's 98.4% payment success rate confirms that payments flow only to registered individuals, not to a broader community. Score = 0 (fully excludable — one class of private actor only).
Q14.src: PM-KISAN Official Notification (eligibility clause); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (98.4% payment success rate, 0.8% rejection rate); pmkisan.gov.in
